pattern: Rooting the tree once and computing, at every node, the best score obtainable by only extending downward into children (discarding any child whose best extension is negative) gives every node's answer relative to that root; re-deriving those downward values as the root conceptually shifts toward each neighbor turns the single-root computation into an answer for every node in one linear pass.
hints: For a fixed root, define each node's "downward" value as its own weight (+1 good, -1 bad) plus the sum, over its children, of that child's downward value but only when it is positive -- a negative-scoring child subtree is simply left out.

The root's own downward value is already its answer, since the whole tree is reachable only through its children; every other node also has a "through the parent" option, contributed by whatever the parent could offer if the current node itself were excluded from the parent's downward sum.

Compute downward values bottom-up from an arbitrary root, then push an "upward" value back down top-down: a child's upward option is the parent's own weight, plus the parent's usable upward value, plus the usable downward values of the parent's other children (its total minus this child's own contribution); a node's final answer is its downward value plus its usable upward value.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a 3-node path with the middle node bad
statement example, a small branching tree
statement example, every node bad so the best subgraph is always just the node itself
smallest possible tree, two good nodes
a star where the center is bad -- leaves should not pull in the whole star
a long path alternating good and bad -- the optimal subgraph for an endpoint should stop short of a losing tail
all nodes good, so every node's best subgraph is the entire tree
a leaf attached to a long bad chain -- the leaf itself should not be pulled into a losing extension

stress.description: random trees built by attaching each node to a random earlier node, mixed good/bad labels
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 700))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([p, i])
    good = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [n, edges, good]

stress.description: random path-shaped and star-shaped trees to stress the rerooting extremes
